title: Building Production-Ready Apps: Testing, Packaging, and Distribution
content:
# Building Production-Ready Apps: Testing, Packaging, and Distribution *Take your OpenMaui Linux applications from development to production with comprehensive testing strategies, packaging for multiple distributions, and deployment best practices.* ## Introduction Developing a .NET MAUI application for Linux is just the beginning. To deliver a professional, production-ready application, you need robust testing, proper packaging for different Linux distributions, and a streamlined deployment pipeline. OpenMaui's comprehensive Linux platform implementation demonstrates how to achieve all of this while maintaining 100% MAUI API compliance. The journey from `dotnet run` to a polished application available in distribution repositories involves several critical steps: unit and integration testing of your Skia-based views, packaging for different distribution formats (.deb, .rpm, Flatpak, Snap, AppImage), setting up continuous integration pipelines, and establishing update mechanisms. Each step presents unique challenges in the Linux ecosystem, where fragmentation across distributions, display servers (X11 vs Wayland), and desktop environments (GNOME, KDE, XFCE) requires careful consideration. This guide walks through the complete production pipeline for OpenMaui applications, drawing from real-world implementation experience with SkiaSharp rendering, GTK integration, and cross-distribution compatibility. Whether you're targeting a single distribution or aiming for universal Linux support, these strategies will help you deliver a reliable, maintainable application. ## Unit Testing Skia Views and Handlers OpenMaui's architecture separates rendering logic from platform integration, making unit testing straightforward. With 217 passing unit tests using xUnit, Moq, and FluentAssertions, the project demonstrates comprehensive test coverage for Skia-based views and handlers. ## Testing View Rendering Logic Skia views like `SkiaButton`, `SkiaLabel`, and `SkiaEntry` contain rendering logic that should be tested independently of the windowing system. Focus your tests on: **Property mapping and updates:** ```csharp [Fact] public void SkiaButton_TextProperty_UpdatesRendering() { var button = new SkiaButton(); button.Text = "Click Me"; Assert.Equal("Click Me", button.Text); Assert.True(button.IsDirty); // Dirty flag triggers redraw } ``` **Visual state management:** ```csharp [Fact] public void SkiaButton_PressedState_ChangesAppearance() { var button = new SkiaButton { BackgroundColor = Colors.Blue }; button.SetVisualState(VisualStates.Pressed); // Verify visual state affects rendering Assert.NotEqual(Colors.Blue, button.CurrentBackgroundColor); } ``` **Layout calculations:** ```csharp [Fact] public void SkiaGrid_MeasureAndArrange_CalculatesCorrectBounds() { var grid = new SkiaGrid { RowDefinitions = new RowDefinitionCollection { new RowDefinition { Height = GridLength.Auto }, new RowDefinition { Height = new GridLength(1, GridUnitType.Star) } } }; var size = grid.Measure(new Size(400, 600)); grid.Arrange(new Rect(0, 0, 400, 600)); Assert.Equal(400, grid.Width); Assert.Equal(600, grid.Height); } ``` ## Testing Handler Implementations Handlers bridge MAUI controls to platform views. Test that property mappers correctly translate MAUI properties to Skia view properties: ```csharp [Fact] public void ButtonHandler_TextProperty_MapsToSkiaButton() { var mauiButton = new Button { Text = "Test" }; var handler = new ButtonHandler(); handler.SetVirtualView(mauiButton); var skiaButton = handler.PlatformView as SkiaButton; Assert.NotNull(skiaButton); Assert.Equal("Test", skiaButton.Text); } [Fact] public void EntryHandler_TextChangedEvent_FiresCorrectly() { var mauiEntry = new Entry(); var handler = new EntryHandler(); handler.SetVirtualView(mauiEntry); string changedText = null; mauiEntry.TextChanged += (s, e) => changedText = e.NewTextValue; var skiaEntry = handler.PlatformView as SkiaEntry; skiaEntry.Text = "New Text"; skiaEntry.RaiseTextChanged(); Assert.Equal("New Text", changedText); } ``` ## Mocking Platform Services Use Moq to isolate platform services during testing: ```csharp [Fact] public async Task FilePicker_PickAsync_ReturnsSelectedFile() { var mockDialogService = new Mock(); mockDialogService .Setup(x => x.ShowFilePickerAsync(It.IsAny())) .ReturnsAsync("/home/user/document.pdf"); var filePicker = new LinuxFilePicker(mockDialogService.Object); var result = await filePicker.PickAsync(); Assert.NotNull(result); Assert.Equal("document.pdf", result.FileName); } ``` ## Test Organization Organize tests by component type: - `Tests/Views/` - Skia view rendering and behavior - `Tests/Handlers/` - Handler property mapping and events - `Tests/Services/` - Platform service implementations - `Tests/Layout/` - Layout container measure and arrange logic With 217 passing unit tests covering views, services, and handlers, OpenMaui demonstrates that comprehensive testing is achievable even for complex platform implementations. ## Integration Testing with Display Servers Unit tests verify logic, but integration tests ensure your application works correctly with X11, Wayland, and different desktop environments. These tests require actual display servers and window managers. ## Setting Up Test Environments **Xvfb for headless X11 testing:** ```bash # Install Xvfb (X Virtual Frame Buffer) sudo apt-get install xvfb # Run tests with virtual display xvfb-run -a dotnet test # With specific resolution and color depth xvfb-run -a -s "-screen 0 1920x1080x24" dotnet test ``` **Wayland testing with weston:** ```bash # Install weston compositor sudo apt-get install weston # Run tests in weston environment weston --backend=headless-backend.so & export WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-0 dotnet test ``` ## Window Lifecycle Tests Test window creation, showing, hiding, and destruction: ```csharp [Fact] public void LinuxWindow_CreateAndShow_DisplaysCorrectly() { var app = new LinuxApplication(); var window = new LinuxWindow(app) { Title = "Test Window", Width = 800, Height = 600 }; window.Show(); app.ProcessEvents(); // Process pending X11/Wayland events Assert.True(window.IsVisible); Assert.Equal(800, window.Width); Assert.Equal(600, window.Height); window.Close(); } ``` ## Rendering Pipeline Tests Verify the SkiaSharp rendering pipeline works with actual GPU contexts: ```csharp [Fact] public void GpuRenderingEngine_Initialize_CreatesOpenGLContext() { var window = new GtkHostWindow(); window.Show(); var renderEngine = new GpuRenderingEngine(window); var initialized = renderEngine.Initialize(); Assert.True(initialized); Assert.NotNull(renderEngine.GRContext); Assert.True(renderEngine.SupportsHardwareAcceleration); window.Close(); } [Fact] public void SkiaRenderingEngine_DirtyRegion_OptimizesRedraws() { var surface = CreateTestSurface(800, 600); var engine = new SkiaRenderingEngine(surface); var button = new SkiaButton { Bounds = new Rect(10, 10, 100, 40) }; engine.AddView(button); engine.Render(); // Initial full render button.Text = "Updated"; var dirtyRect = engine.GetDirtyRegion(); // Only button region should be dirty Assert.Equal(new Rect(10, 10, 100, 40), dirtyRect); } ``` ## Multi-Monitor Tests Test HiDPI scaling and multi-monitor configurations: ```csharp [Fact] public void LinuxWindow_HiDPIDisplay_ScalesCorrectly() { var window = new LinuxWindow(new LinuxApplication()); var scaleFactor = window.GetScaleFactor(); window.Width = 800; window.Height = 600; // Physical pixels should account for scale factor Assert.Equal(800 * scaleFactor, window.PhysicalWidth); Assert.Equal(600 * scaleFactor, window.PhysicalHeight); } ``` ## Input Method Editor (IME) Tests Test text input with IBus, Fcitx5, and XIM: ```csharp [Fact] public void SkiaEntry_IMEInput_HandlesComposition() { var entry = new SkiaEntry(); var imeContext = new LinuxIMEContext(entry); // Simulate IME composition imeContext.SetCompositionText("こんにちは"); Assert.Equal("こんにちは", entry.CompositionText); // Commit composition imeContext.CommitComposition(); Assert.Equal("こんにちは", entry.Text); Assert.Null(entry.CompositionText); } ``` ## Theme Detection Tests Verify system theme detection across desktop environments: ```csharp [Theory] [InlineData("GNOME", "Adwaita-dark")] [InlineData("KDE", "Breeze-Dark")] public void SystemThemeService_DetectDarkMode_ReturnsCorrectTheme( string desktopEnv, string themeName) { Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP", desktopEnv); Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("GTK_THEME", themeName); var themeService = new SystemThemeService(); var isDark = themeService.IsDarkMode(); Assert.True(isDark); } ``` ## Continuous Integration Setup Configure GitHub Actions for automated integration testing: ```yaml name: Integration Tests on: [push, pull_request] jobs: test: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: Setup .NET uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3 with: dotnet-version: '8.0.x' - name: Install dependencies run: | sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y xvfb libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 - name: Run integration tests run: xvfb-run -a dotnet test --filter Category=Integration ``` Integration tests catch issues that unit tests miss: display server compatibility, font rendering with HarfBuzz, gesture recognition accuracy, and theme application consistency. ## Packaging as .deb and .rpm Debian (.deb) and RPM packages are the foundation of Linux distribution, used by Ubuntu/Debian and Fedora/RHEL families respectively. Proper packaging ensures your application integrates seamlessly with system package managers. ## Debian Package Structure Create a `debian/` directory in your project root: ``` debian/ ├── changelog ├── control ├── copyright ├── rules ├── openmaui-app.install └── openmaui-app.desktop ``` **debian/control** - Package metadata: ``` Source: openmaui-app Section: misc Priority: optional Maintainer: Your Name Build-Depends: debhelper (>= 12), dotnet-sdk-8.0 Standards-Version: 4.5.0 Package: openmaui-app Architecture: amd64 Depends: ${shlibs:Depends}, ${misc:Depends}, libgtk-3-0 (>= 3.24), libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37, libskia, libharfbuzz0b, aspnetcore-runtime-8.0 Description: OpenMaui Linux Application A cross-platform .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux with full Skia rendering and GTK integration. ``` **debian/rules** - Build instructions: ```makefile #!/usr/bin/make -f %: dh $@ override_dh_auto_build: dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \ --self-contained false \ -p:PublishSingleFile=false \ -o debian/tmp/usr/lib/openmaui-app override_dh_auto_install: dh_auto_install install -D -m 644 debian/openmaui-app.desktop \ debian/openmaui-app/usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop install -D -m 644 Resources/appicon.png \ debian/openmaui-app/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png ``` **debian/openmaui-app.install** - File installation paths: ``` usr/lib/openmaui-app/* usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png ``` **debian/changelog** - Version history: ``` openmaui-app (1.0.0-1) unstable; urgency=medium * Initial release * Full MAUI API compliance * X11 and Wayland support -- Your Name Mon, 20 Jan 2025 10:00:00 +0000 ``` ## Building the .deb Package ```bash # Install build tools sudo apt-get install debhelper devscripts # Build package dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b # Result: ../openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_amd64.deb # Test installation sudo dpkg -i ../openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_amd64.deb sudo apt-get install -f # Fix dependencies if needed ``` ## RPM Package Structure Create an RPM spec file `openmaui-app.spec`: ```spec Name: openmaui-app Version: 1.0.0 Release: 1%{?dist} Summary: OpenMaui Linux Application License: MIT URL: https://github.com/yourusername/openmaui-app Source0: %{name}-%{version}.tar.gz BuildRequires: dotnet-sdk-8.0 Requires: gtk3 >= 3.24 Requires: webkit2gtk3 Requires: aspnetcore-runtime-8.0 Requires: libSkiaSharp Requires: harfbuzz %description A cross-platform .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux with full Skia rendering and GTK integration. %prep %setup -q %build dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \ --self-contained false \ -p:PublishSingleFile=false \ -o %{buildroot}/usr/lib/%{name} %install rm -rf %{buildroot} mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/lib/%{name} mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/share/applications mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps cp -r bin/Release/net8.0/linux-x64/publish/* %{buildroot}/usr/lib/%{name}/ install -m 644 openmaui-app.desktop %{buildroot}/usr/share/applications/ install -m 644 Resources/appicon.png %{buildroot}/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/%{name}.png %files /usr/lib/%{name}/ /usr/share/applications/%{name}.desktop /usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/%{name}.png %changelog * Mon Jan 20 2025 Your Name - 1.0.0-1 - Initial release ``` ## Building the RPM Package ```bash # Install build tools (Fedora/RHEL) sudo dnf install rpm-build rpmdevtools # Setup RPM build environment rpmdev-setuptree # Copy spec and source cp openmaui-app.spec ~/rpmbuild/SPECS/ tar czf ~/rpmbuild/SOURCES/openmaui-app-1.0.0.tar.gz . # Build RPM rpmbuild -ba ~/rpmbuild/SPECS/openmaui-app.spec # Result: ~/rpmbuild/RPMS/x86_64/openmaui-app-1.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm # Test installation sudo dnf install ~/rpmbuild/RPMS/x86_64/openmaui-app-1.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm ``` ## Desktop Entry File Create `openmaui-app.desktop` for both package types: ```ini [Desktop Entry] Version=1.0 Type=Application Name=OpenMaui App Comment=Cross-platform .NET MAUI application Exec=/usr/lib/openmaui-app/OpenMauiApp Icon=openmaui-app Terminal=false Categories=Utility;Development; Keywords=maui;dotnet;cross-platform; StartupWMClass=OpenMauiApp ``` ## Dependency Management OpenMaui applications require specific runtime dependencies: **Core dependencies:** - `aspnetcore-runtime-8.0` - .NET runtime - `libgtk-3-0` / `gtk3` - GTK windowing - `libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37` / `webkit2gtk3` - WebView support - `libskia` - SkiaSharp native libraries - `libharfbuzz0b` / `harfbuzz` - Text shaping **Optional dependencies:** - `libva2` - Hardware video acceleration - `at-spi2-core` - Accessibility support - `ibus` / `fcitx5` - Input method support Test your packages on clean VMs to verify all dependencies are correctly specified. ## Flatpak and Snap Distribution Flatpak and Snap provide sandboxed, distribution-agnostic packaging with automatic updates. They're ideal for reaching users across all Linux distributions without maintaining separate .deb and .rpm packages. ## Flatpak Packaging Flatpak uses manifest files to define your application and its dependencies. **com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml** - Flatpak manifest: ```yaml app-id: com.example.OpenMauiApp runtime: org.freedesktop.Platform runtime-version: '23.08' sdk: org.freedesktop.Sdk sdk-extensions: - org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.dotnet8 command: openmaui-app finish-args: # Wayland and X11 access - --socket=wayland - --socket=fallback-x11 - --share=ipc # GPU acceleration - --device=dri # File system access - --filesystem=home - --filesystem=xdg-documents # Network access (for WebView) - --share=network # Desktop integration - --talk-name=org.freedesktop.Notifications - --talk-name=org.freedesktop.secrets - --talk-name=org.gtk.vfs.* # Theme access - --filesystem=xdg-config/gtk-3.0:ro - --env=GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark build-options: append-path: /usr/lib/sdk/dotnet8/bin env: PKG_CONFIG_PATH: /app/lib/pkgconfig:/app/share/pkgconfig:/usr/lib/pkgconfig:/usr/share/pkgconfig modules: # SkiaSharp native dependencies - name: skia buildsystem: simple build-commands: - install -Dm755 libSkiaSharp.so /app/lib/libSkiaSharp.so sources: - type: file url: https://www.nuget.org/api/v2/package/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux/2.88.9 sha256: # HarfBuzz for text shaping - name: harfbuzz buildsystem: meson config-opts: - -Dglib=enabled - -Dfreetype=enabled sources: - type: archive url: https://github.com/harfbuzz/harfbuzz/releases/download/7.3.0/harfbuzz-7.3.0.tar.xz sha256: # Main application - name: openmaui-app buildsystem: simple build-commands: - dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained false -o /app/bin - install -Dm644 com.example.OpenMauiApp.desktop /app/share/applications/com.example.OpenMauiApp.desktop - install -Dm644 Resources/appicon.png /app/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/com.example.OpenMauiApp.png - install -Dm644 com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml /app/share/metainfo/com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml sources: - type: dir path: . ``` **Building and testing Flatpak:** ```bash # Install flatpak-builder sudo apt-get install flatpak-builder # Add Flathub repository flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo # Install required runtimes flatpak install flathub org.freedesktop.Platform//23.08 flatpak install flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk//23.08 flatpak install flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.dotnet8//23.08 # Build Flatpak flatpak-builder --force-clean build-dir com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml # Test locally flatpak-builder --run build-dir com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml openmaui-app # Create single-file bundle for distribution flatpak-builder --repo=repo --force-clean build-dir com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml flatpak build-bundle repo openmaui-app.flatpak com.example.OpenMauiApp # Install bundle flatpak install openmaui-app.flatpak ``` **AppStream metadata** (com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml): ```xml com.example.OpenMauiApp CC0-1.0 MIT OpenMaui App Cross-platform .NET MAUI application

A production-ready .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux with full Skia rendering, GTK integration, and cross-platform compatibility.

https://example.com/screenshots/main.png https://example.com ``` ## Snap Packaging Snap uses a `snapcraft.yaml` file for configuration. **snapcraft.yaml:** ```yaml name: openmaui-app base: core22 version: '1.0.0' summary: OpenMaui Linux Application description: | A cross-platform .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux with full Skia rendering and GTK integration. grade: stable confinement: strict apps: openmaui-app: command: bin/openmaui-app extensions: [gnome] plugs: - home - network - desktop - desktop-legacy - wayland - x11 - opengl - audio-playback - removable-media parts: dotnet-runtime: plugin: nil build-packages: - wget override-build: | wget https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh chmod +x dotnet-install.sh ./dotnet-install.sh --channel 8.0 --runtime aspnetcore --install-dir $SNAPCRAFT_PART_INSTALL/dotnet stage: - dotnet/* openmaui-app: plugin: dotnet source: . dotnet-build-configuration: Release dotnet-self-contained-runtime-identifier: linux-x64 build-packages: - dotnet-sdk-8.0 stage-packages: - libgtk-3-0 - libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 - libharfbuzz0b - libfontconfig1 - libfreetype6 override-build: | dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \ --self-contained false \ -o $SNAPCRAFT_PART_INSTALL/bin ``` **Building and publishing Snap:** ```bash # Install snapcraft sudo snap install snapcraft --classic # Build snap snapcraft # Test locally sudo snap install openmaui-app_1.0.0_amd64.snap --dangerous # Run application openmaui-app # Publish to Snap Store (requires account) snapcraft login snapcraft upload openmaui-app_1.0.0_amd64.snap --release=stable ``` ## Portal Permissions Both Flatpak and Snap use portals for secure file access: ```csharp // OpenMaui handles portals automatically public class LinuxFilePicker : IFilePicker { public async Task PickAsync(PickOptions options) { // Detects if running in sandbox if (IsRunningInFlatpak() || IsRunningInSnap()) { // Use xdg-desktop-portal for sandboxed file access return await PickViaPortalAsync(options); } else { // Direct file picker (zenity/kdialog) return await PickDirectAsync(options); } } private bool IsRunningInFlatpak() { return File.Exists("/.flatpak-info"); } private bool IsRunningInSnap() { return !string.IsNullOrEmpty(Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("SNAP")); } } ``` ## Comparison: Flatpak vs Snap **Flatpak advantages:** - Better desktop integration - More granular permissions - Preferred by traditional Linux distributions - Excellent for GTK applications **Snap advantages:** - Simpler manifest syntax - Better CLI tool support - Strong Ubuntu/Canonical ecosystem - Automatic delta updates For OpenMaui applications, **Flatpak is recommended** due to superior GTK integration and broader community adoption. ## AppImage for Universal Compatibility AppImage provides the simplest distribution method: a single executable file that runs on any Linux distribution without installation. The beauty of AppImage is its simplicity: bundle everything your application needs into a single executable file that runs on virtually any Linux distribution without installation. ## Creating an AppImage AppImages use the AppDir structure with all dependencies bundled: ```bash # Install appimagetool wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage chmod +x appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage # Create AppDir structure mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/bin mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/lib mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/share/applications mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps ``` ## Publishing Self-Contained Build Publish your application with all dependencies: ```bash dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \ --self-contained true \ -p:PublishSingleFile=false \ -p:PublishTrimmed=false \ -o publish/ # Copy to AppDir cp -r publish/* OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/bin/ ``` ## Bundling Native Dependencies Copy required native libraries: ```bash #!/bin/bash # bundle-dependencies.sh APPDIR="OpenMauiApp.AppDir" LIBDIR="$APPDIR/usr/lib" # Copy GTK and dependencies cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgtk-3.so.0 $LIBDIR/ cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgdk-3.so.0 $LIBDIR/ cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libwebkit2gtk-4.0.so.37 $LIBDIR/ # Copy SkiaSharp native cp ~/.nuget/packages/skiasharp.nativeassets.linux/2.88.9/runtimes/linux-x64/native/libSkiaSharp.so $LIBDIR/ # Copy HarfBuzz cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libharfbuzz.so.0 $LIBDIR/ # Copy their dependencies for lib in $LIBDIR/*.so*; do ldd $lib | grep "=> /" | awk '{print $3}' | while read dep; do if [ ! -f "$LIBDIR/$(basename $dep)" ]; then cp $dep $LIBDIR/ fi done done ``` ## AppRun Script Create the launcher script `OpenMauiApp.AppDir/AppRun`: ```bash #!/bin/bash # Get the directory of this script HERE="$(dirname "$(readlink -f "${0}")")" # Set library path to include bundled libraries export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH" # Set GTK theme path export GTK_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib/gtk-3.0" export GTK_DATA_PREFIX="$HERE/usr" # Set GDK backend (prefer Wayland, fallback to X11) export GDK_BACKEND=wayland,x11 # Disable AppImage desktop integration (optional) export APPIMAGE_DISABLE_INTEGRATION=1 # Run the application exec "$HERE/usr/bin/OpenMauiApp" "$@" ``` Make it executable: ```bash chmod +x OpenMauiApp.AppDir/AppRun ``` ## Desktop Entry and Icon **OpenMauiApp.AppDir/openmaui-app.desktop:** ```ini [Desktop Entry] Type=Application Name=OpenMaui App Exec=openmaui-app Icon=openmaui-app Categories=Utility;Development; Terminal=false ``` Copy icon and create symlinks: ```bash cp Resources/appicon.png OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png cp Resources/appicon.png OpenMauiApp.AppDir/openmaui-app.png cp Resources/appicon.png OpenMauiApp.AppDir/.DirIcon ln -s usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop OpenMauiApp.AppDir/openmaui-app.desktop ``` ## Building the AppImage ```bash # Build AppImage ./appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage OpenMauiApp.AppDir OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage # Make executable chmod +x OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage # Test ./OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage ``` ## AppImage Updates Implement AppImageUpdate support by embedding update information: ```bash # Add update information to AppImage ./appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage OpenMauiApp.AppDir \ OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage \ -u "gh-releases-zsync|yourusername|openmaui-app|latest|OpenMauiApp-*-x86_64.AppImage.zsync" ``` Generate zsync file for delta updates: ```bash zsyncmake OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage ``` ## Runtime Update Checks Implement update checking in your application: ```csharp public class AppImageUpdateService { public async Task CheckForUpdatesAsync() { var appImagePath = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("APPIMAGE"); if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(appImagePath)) return false; // Not running as AppImage var process = new Process { StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo { FileName = "appimageupdatetool", Arguments = $"-j {appImagePath}", RedirectStandardOutput = true, UseShellExecute = false } }; process.Start(); var output = await process.StandardOutput.ReadToEndAsync(); await process.WaitForExitAsync(); var updateInfo = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(output); return updateInfo?.UpdateAvailable ?? false; } public async Task ApplyUpdateAsync() { var appImagePath = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("APPIMAGE"); var process = new Process { StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo { FileName = "appimageupdatetool", Arguments = appImagePath, UseShellExecute = true } }; process.Start(); await process.WaitForExitAsync(); // Restart application Process.Start(appImagePath); Environment.Exit(0); } } ``` ## Automated AppImage Build Script Create `build-appimage.sh`: ```bash #!/bin/bash set -e VERSION="1.0.0" APPNAME="OpenMauiApp" APPDIR="$APPNAME.AppDir" echo "Building $APPNAME v$VERSION AppImage..." # Clean previous build rm -rf $APPDIR rm -f $APPNAME-*.AppImage # Publish application dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained true -o publish/ # Create AppDir structure mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/bin mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/lib mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/share/applications mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps # Copy application cp -r publish/* $APPDIR/usr/bin/ # Bundle dependencies ./bundle-dependencies.sh # Copy desktop entry and icon cp openmaui-app.desktop $APPDIR/usr/share/applications/ cp Resources/appicon.png $APPDIR/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png cp Resources/appicon.png $APPDIR/openmaui-app.png cp Resources/appicon.png $APPDIR/.DirIcon ln -s usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop $APPDIR/openmaui-app.desktop # Create AppRun cat > $APPDIR/AppRun << 'EOF' #!/bin/bash HERE="$(dirname "$(readlink -f "${0}")")" export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH" export GTK_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib/gtk-3.0" exec "$HERE/usr/bin/OpenMauiApp" "$@" EOF chmod +x $APPDIR/AppRun # Build AppImage ./appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage $APPDIR $APPNAME-$VERSION-x86_64.AppImage echo "AppImage created: $APPNAME-$VERSION-x86_64.AppImage" echo "Size: $(du -h $APPNAME-$VERSION-x86_64.AppImage | cut -f1)" ``` AppImages are perfect for rapid distribution, beta testing, and users who prefer not to use package managers. ## CI/CD Pipeline Setup Automated build, test, and release pipelines ensure consistent quality and streamline distribution across multiple package formats. Here's how to set up comprehensive CI/CD for OpenMaui Linux applications. ## GitHub Actions Workflow Create `.github/workflows/release.yml`: ```yaml name: Build and Release on: push: branches: [main] tags: - 'v*' pull_request: branches: [main] env: DOTNET_VERSION: '8.0.x' PROJECT_NAME: OpenMauiApp jobs: test: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: Setup .NET uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3 with: dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }} - name: Install dependencies run: | sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y xvfb libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 \ libharfbuzz0b libfontconfig1 libfreetype6 - name: Restore dependencies run: dotnet restore - name: Build run: dotnet build -c Release --no-restore - name: Run unit tests run: dotnet test -c Release --no-build --verbosity normal \ --filter Category=Unit - name: Run integration tests run: xvfb-run -a dotnet test -c Release --no-build --verbosity normal \ --filter Category=Integration build-deb: needs: test runs-on: ubuntu-latest if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: Setup .NET uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3 with: dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }} - name: Install build tools run: sudo apt-get install -y debhelper devscripts - name: Build .deb package run: | dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b mv ../*.deb . - name: Upload .deb artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3 with: name: deb-package path: '*.deb' build-rpm: needs: test runs-on: ubuntu-latest if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') container: image: fedora:latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: Install dependencies run: | dnf install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0 rpm-build rpmdevtools - name: Setup RPM build tree run: rpmdev-setuptree - name: Build RPM run: | cp openmaui-app.spec ~/rpmbuild/SPECS/ tar czf ~/rpmbuild/SOURCES/${{ env.PROJECT_NAME }}-${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}.tar.gz . rpmbuild -ba ~/rpmbuild/SPECS/openmaui-app.spec cp ~/rpmbuild/RPMS/x86_64/*.rpm . - name: Upload RPM artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3 with: name: rpm-package path: '*.rpm' build-flatpak: needs: test runs-on: ubuntu-latest if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: Install Flatpak and flatpak-builder run: | sudo apt-get install -y flatpak flatpak-builder flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo flatpak install -y flathub org.freedesktop.Platform//23.08 flatpak install -y flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk//23.08 flatpak install -y flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.dotnet8//23.08 - name: Build Flatpak run: | flatpak-builder --repo=repo --force-clean build-dir \ com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml flatpak build-bundle repo ${{ env.PROJECT_NAME }}.flatpak \ com.example.OpenMauiApp - name: Upload Flatpak artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3 with: name: flatpak-package path: '*.flatpak' build-appimage: needs: test runs-on: ubuntu-latest if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: Setup .NET uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3 with: dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }} - name: Install dependencies run: | sudo apt-get install -y libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 \ libharfbuzz0b zsync wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage chmod +x appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage - name: Build AppImage run: | chmod +x build-appimage.sh ./build-appimage.sh zsyncmake ${{ env.PROJECT_NAME }}-*.AppImage - name: Upload AppImage artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3 with: name: appimage-package path: | *.AppImage *.AppImage.zsync release: needs: [build-deb, build-rpm, build-flatpak, build-appimage] runs-on: ubuntu-latest if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') steps: - name: Download all artifacts uses: actions/download-artifact@v3 - name: Create Release uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1 with: files: | deb-package/*.deb rpm-package/*.rpm flatpak-package/*.flatpak appimage-package/*.AppImage appimage-package/*.AppImage.zsync draft: false prerelease: false env: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} ``` ## GitLab CI/CD Pipeline For GitLab, create `.gitlab-ci.yml`: ```yaml stages: - test - build - release variables: DOTNET_VERSION: "8.0" PROJECT_NAME: "OpenMauiApp" test:unit: stage: test image: mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 before_script: - apt-get update - apt-get install -y libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 script: - dotnet restore - dotnet build -c Release - dotnet test -c Release --filter Category=Unit test:integration: stage: test image: mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 before_script: - apt-get update - apt-get install -y xvfb libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 script: - xvfb-run -a dotnet test -c Release --filter Category=Integration build:deb: stage: build image: ubuntu:22.04 only: - tags script: - apt-get update - apt-get install -y debhelper devscripts dotnet-sdk-8.0 - dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b - mv ../*.deb . artifacts: paths: - "*.deb" build:appimage: stage: build image: ubuntu:22.04 only: - tags script: - apt-get update - apt-get install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0 libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 wget - wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage - chmod +x appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage build-appimage.sh - ./build-appimage.sh artifacts: paths: - "*.AppImage" release: stage: release image: registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/release-cli:latest only: - tags script: - echo "Creating release $CI_COMMIT_TAG" release: tag_name: $CI_COMMIT_TAG description: "Release $CI_COMMIT_TAG" assets: links: - name: "Debian Package" url: "${CI_PROJECT_URL}/-/jobs/artifacts/${CI_COMMIT_TAG}/raw/*.deb?job=build:deb" - name: "AppImage" url: "${CI_PROJECT_URL}/-/jobs/artifacts/${CI_COMMIT_TAG}/raw/*.AppImage?job=build:appimage" ``` ## Version Management Automate version bumping in `.csproj`: ```xml net8.0 1.0.0 1.0.0.0 1.0.0.0 1.0.$(BUILD_NUMBER) ``` ## Automated Testing Matrix Test across multiple distributions: ```yaml test-matrix: strategy: matrix: os: - ubuntu-22.04 - ubuntu-20.04 display-server: - x11 - wayland runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }} steps: - name: Setup display server run: | if [ "${{ matrix.display-server }}" = "wayland" ]; then sudo apt-get install -y weston weston --backend=headless-backend.so & export WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-0 else xvfb-run -a dotnet test fi ``` ## Code Signing Sign packages for distribution: ```bash # GPG signing for .deb packages dpkg-sig --sign builder openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_amd64.deb # RPM signing rpmsign --addsign openmaui-app-1.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm # AppImage signing gpg --detach-sign --armor OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage ``` Store signing keys in CI secrets and automate signing in the pipeline. ## Debugging in WSL vs Native Linux Developing on Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) offers convenience, but debugging production issues often requires native Linux environments. Understanding the differences helps you debug effectively in both contexts. ## WSL Development Setup WSL 2 with WSLg provides GUI support for Linux applications: ```bash # Check WSL version wsl --version # Ensure WSL 2 is enabled wsl --set-default-version 2 # Install Ubuntu wsl --install -d Ubuntu-22.04 ``` **Install development dependencies:** ```bash sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0 libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 \ libharfbuzz0b libfontconfig1 libfreetype6 ``` ## Visual Studio Code Remote Development Configure VS Code for WSL debugging: **.vscode/launch.json:** ```json { "version": "0.2.0", "configurations": [ { "name": "Launch OpenMaui (WSL)", "type": "coreclr", "request": "launch", "preLaunchTask": "build", "program": "${workspaceFolder}/bin/Debug/net8.0/OpenMauiApp.dll", "args": [], "cwd": "${workspaceFolder}", "console": "internalConsole", "stopAtEntry": false, "env": { "DISPLAY": ":0", "WAYLAND_DISPLAY": "wayland-0" } }, { "name": "Attach to Process (WSL)", "type": "coreclr", "request": "attach", "processId": "${command:pickProcess}" } ] } ``` ## WSL-Specific Issues **Display server differences:** WSLg uses Weston as the Wayland compositor, which may behave differently from GNOME or KDE: ```csharp public class DisplayServerDetector { public static bool IsRunningInWSL() { try { var version = File.ReadAllText("/proc/version"); return version.Contains("microsoft", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase); } catch { return false; } } public static void LogEnvironment() { Console.WriteLine($"WSL: {IsRunningInWSL()}"); Console.WriteLine($"DISPLAY: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("DISPLAY")}"); Console.WriteLine($"WAYLAND_DISPLAY: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("WAYLAND_DISPLAY")}"); Console.WriteLine($"XDG_SESSION_TYPE: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("XDG_SESSION_TYPE")}"); } } ``` **Font rendering:** WSL may have limited font fallback support. Test with emoji and CJK characters: ```csharp [Fact] public void SkiaLabel_ComplexText_RendersCorrectly() { var label = new SkiaLabel { Text = "Hello 世界 🌍 مرحبا" }; var surface = CreateTestSurface(400, 100); label.Measure(new Size(400, 100)); label.Arrange(new Rect(0, 0, 400, 100)); label.Draw(surface.Canvas); // Verify text was rendered (not just boxes) var bitmap = surface.PeekPixels(); Assert.True(HasNonWhitePixels(bitmap)); } ``` **Hardware acceleration:** WSLg provides limited GPU access. Detect and fallback gracefully: ```csharp public class RenderEngineFactory { public static ISkiaRenderingEngine Create(IWindow window) { if (DisplayServerDetector.IsRunningInWSL()) { // WSL: Prefer software rendering Console.WriteLine("WSL detected, using software rendering"); return new SkiaRenderingEngine(window); } // Native Linux: Try GPU first try { var gpuEngine = new GpuRenderingEngine(window); if (gpuEngine.Initialize()) { Console.WriteLine("Using GPU-accelerated rendering"); return gpuEngine; } } catch (Exception ex) { Console.WriteLine($"GPU initialization failed: {ex.Message}"); } Console.WriteLine("Falling back to software rendering"); return new SkiaRenderingEngine(window); } } ``` ## Native Linux Debugging Testing in WSL provides convenience during development, but native Linux testing is essential for catching display server quirks, font rendering issues, and hardware acceleration problems. **SSH remote debugging:** ```bash # On Linux machine, install SSH server sudo apt-get install openssh-server sudo systemctl start ssh # From Windows, connect via VS Code Remote-SSH # Or use dotnet trace for profiling ssh user@linux-machine dotnet trace collect --process-id $(pgrep OpenMauiApp) ``` **Remote debugging with lldb:** ```bash # Install lldb sudo apt-get install lldb # Attach to running process sudo lldb -p $(pgrep OpenMauiApp) # Set breakpoint (lldb) breakpoint set --name SkiaButton.OnDraw (lldb) continue ``` ## Logging and Diagnostics Implement comprehensive logging for debugging: ```csharp public static class DiagnosticLogger { private static readonly string LogPath = Path.Combine( Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData), "OpenMauiApp", "logs", $"app-{DateTime.Now:yyyyMMdd-HHmmss}.log" ); static DiagnosticLogger() { Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.GetDirectoryName(LogPath)); } public static void LogEnvironment() { var sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.AppendLine("=== Environment Information ==="); sb.AppendLine($"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}"); sb.AppendLine($"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}"); sb.AppendLine($"WSL: {DisplayServerDetector.IsRunningInWSL()}"); sb.AppendLine($"Display: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("DISPLAY")}"); sb.AppendLine($"Session Type: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("XDG_SESSION_TYPE")}"); sb.AppendLine($"Desktop: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP")}"); sb.AppendLine($"Theme: {Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("GTK_THEME")}"); File.AppendAllText(LogPath, sb.ToString()); } public static void LogException(Exception ex, string context) { var sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.AppendLine($"[{DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}] ERROR in {context}"); sb.AppendLine($"Message: {ex.Message}"); sb.AppendLine($"Stack Trace:\n{ex.StackTrace}"); if (ex.InnerException != null) { sb.AppendLine($"Inner Exception: {ex.InnerException.Message}"); } File.AppendAllText(LogPath, sb.ToString()); } } ``` ## Performance Profiling Compare performance between WSL and native Linux: ```csharp public class PerformanceBenchmark { [Benchmark] public void RenderComplexLayout() { var grid = CreateComplexGrid(); var surface = CreateTestSurface(1920, 1080); var stopwatch = Stopwatch.StartNew(); grid.Measure(new Size(1920, 1080)); grid.Arrange(new Rect(0, 0, 1920, 1080)); grid.Draw(surface.Canvas); stopwatch.Stop(); Console.WriteLine($"Render time: {stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds}ms"); Console.WriteLine($"Environment: {(DisplayServerDetector.IsRunningInWSL() ? "WSL" : "Native")}"); } } ``` ## Testing Checklist **WSL testing:** - ✓ Basic functionality - ✓ UI layout and rendering - ✓ Unit and integration tests - ✓ Rapid iteration during development **Native Linux testing:** - ✓ Multiple distributions (Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch) - ✓ Different desktop environments (GNOME, KDE, XFCE) - ✓ X11 and Wayland display servers - ✓ Hardware acceleration - ✓ Font rendering with complex scripts - ✓ System theme integration - ✓ Multi-monitor configurations - ✓ HiDPI scaling - ✓ Performance profiling Use WSL for day-to-day development, but always validate on native Linux before release. ## Distribution and Update Strategies Getting your application into users' hands is just the beginning. A robust distribution and update strategy ensures users stay on the latest version with minimal friction. ## Distribution Channels ### Official Distribution Repositories **Ubuntu PPA (Personal Package Archive):** ```bash # Create Launchpad account and PPA # Upload source package dput ppa:yourusername/openmaui-app openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_source.changes # Users install via: sudo add-apt-repository ppa:yourusername/openmaui-app sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install openmaui-app ``` **Fedora COPR (Cool Other Package Repo):** ```bash # Create COPR project at copr.fedorainfracloud.org copr-cli create openmaui-app --chroot fedora-39-x86_64 # Build package copr-cli build openmaui-app openmaui-app-1.0.0-1.src.rpm # Users install via: sudo dnf copr enable yourusername/openmaui-app sudo dnf install openmaui-app ``` **Arch User Repository (AUR):** Create `PKGBUILD`: ```bash # Maintainer: Your Name pkgname=openmaui-app pkgver=1.0.0 pkgrel=1 pkgdesc="OpenMaui Linux Application" arch=('x86_64') url="https://github.com/yourusername/openmaui-app" license=('MIT') depends=('dotnet-runtime' 'gtk3' 'webkit2gtk' 'harfbuzz') makdepends=('dotnet-sdk') source=("$pkgname-$pkgver.tar.gz::$url/archive/v$pkgver.tar.gz") sha256sums=('SKIP') build() { cd "$pkgname-$pkgver" dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained false -o out } package() { cd "$pkgname-$pkgver" install -dm755 "$pkgdir/usr/lib/$pkgname" cp -r out/* "$pkgdir/usr/lib/$pkgname/" install -Dm644 "openmaui-app.desktop" "$pkgdir/usr/share/applications/$pkgname.desktop" install -Dm644 "Resources/appicon.png" "$pkgdir/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/$pkgname.png" } ``` ### Flathub Submission Submit your Flatpak to Flathub for centralized distribution: ```bash # Fork flathub/flathub repository git clone https://github.com/flathub/flathub.git cd flathub # Create application directory mkdir com.example.OpenMauiApp cd com.example.OpenMauiApp # Add manifest and metadata cp ~/openmaui-app/com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml . cp ~/openmaui-app/com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml . # Submit pull request git add . git commit -m "Add OpenMaui App" git push origin main ``` ## Automatic Update Mechanisms ### In-App Update Checker Implement update checking for AppImage and standalone installations: ```csharp public class UpdateService { private const string UpdateCheckUrl = "https://api.github.com/repos/yourusername/openmaui-app/releases/latest"; private readonly HttpClient _httpClient; public UpdateService() { _httpClient = new HttpClient(); _httpClient.DefaultRequestHeaders.UserAgent.ParseAdd("OpenMauiApp/1.0"); } public async Task CheckForUpdatesAsync() { try { var response = await _httpClient.GetStringAsync(UpdateCheckUrl); var release = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(response); var currentVersion = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetName().Version; var latestVersion = Version.Parse(release.TagName.TrimStart('v')); return new UpdateInfo { UpdateAvailable = latestVersion > currentVersion, CurrentVersion = currentVersion.ToString(), LatestVersion = latestVersion.ToString(), ReleaseNotes = release.Body, DownloadUrl = GetDownloadUrl(release) }; } catch (Exception ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Update check failed: {ex.Message}"); return new UpdateInfo { UpdateAvailable = false }; } } private string GetDownloadUrl(GitHubRelease release) { // Detect installation type and return appropriate download URL if (IsAppImage()) return release.Assets.FirstOrDefault(a => a.Name.EndsWith(".AppImage"))?.BrowserDownloadUrl; else if (IsDebian()) return release.Assets.FirstOrDefault(a => a.Name.EndsWith(".deb"))?.BrowserDownloadUrl; else if (IsFedora()) return release.Assets.FirstOrDefault(a => a.Name.EndsWith(".rpm"))?.BrowserDownloadUrl; return release.Assets.FirstOrDefault()?.BrowserDownloadUrl; } private bool IsAppImage() => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("APPIMAGE")); private bool IsDebian() => File.Exists("/etc/debian_version"); private bool IsFedora() => File.Exists("/etc/fedora-release"); } public class UpdateInfo { public bool UpdateAvailable { get; set; } public string CurrentVersion { get; set; } public string LatestVersion { get; set; } public string ReleaseNotes { get; set; } public string DownloadUrl { get; set; } } public class GitHubRelease { [JsonPropertyName("tag_name")] public string TagName { get; set; } [JsonPropertyName("body")] public string Body { get; set; } [JsonPropertyName("assets")] public List Assets { get; set; } } public class GitHubAsset { [JsonPropertyName("name")] public string Name { get; set; } [JsonPropertyName("browser_download_url")] public string BrowserDownloadUrl { get; set; } } ``` ### Update UI Integration Show update notifications in your application: ```csharp public class MainPage : ContentPage { private readonly UpdateService _updateService; public MainPage() { InitializeComponent(); _updateService = new UpdateService(); // Check for updates on startup (after short delay) Dispatcher.DispatchDelayed(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5), async () => { await CheckForUpdatesAsync(); }); } private async Task CheckForUpdatesAsync() { var updateInfo = await _updateService.CheckForUpdatesAsync(); if (updateInfo.UpdateAvailable) { var result = await DisplayAlert( "Update Available", $"Version {updateInfo.LatestVersion} is available. " + $"You are currently running version {updateInfo.CurrentVersion}.\n\n" + $"Release Notes:\n{updateInfo.ReleaseNotes}\n\n" + "Would you like to download the update?", "Download", "Later" ); if (result) { await Launcher.OpenAsync(updateInfo.DownloadUrl); } } } } ``` ### Flatpak/Snap Automatic Updates Flatpak and Snap handle updates automatically: **Flatpak:** ```bash # Users can manually update flatpak update com.example.OpenMauiApp # Or enable automatic updates flatpak update --assumeyes # Configure systemd timer for automatic updates systemctl --user enable flatpak-update.timer ``` **Snap:** ```bash # Snap updates automatically by default # Users can manually trigger sudo snap refresh openmaui-app # Check update status snap refresh --list ``` ## Analytics and Telemetry Track version adoption (with user consent): ```csharp public class TelemetryService { private const string TelemetryEndpoint = "https://telemetry.example.com/api/events"; public async Task ReportAppStartAsync() { if (!HasUserConsent()) return; var telemetry = new { EventType = "app_start", Version = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetName().Version.ToString(), OS = Environment.OSVersion.ToString(), Distribution = GetLinuxDistribution(), DesktopEnvironment = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP"), DisplayServer = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("XDG_SESSION_TYPE"), InstallationType = GetInstallationType(), Timestamp = DateTime.UtcNow }; try { var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(telemetry); var content = new StringContent(json, Encoding.UTF8, "application/json"); await new HttpClient().PostAsync(TelemetryEndpoint, content); } catch { // Silently fail - telemetry should never break the app } } private string GetInstallationType() { if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("APPIMAGE"))) return "AppImage"; if (File.Exists("/.flatpak-info")) return "Flatpak"; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("SNAP"))) return "Snap"; if (File.Exists("/var/lib/dpkg/status")) return "Deb"; if (File.Exists("/var/lib/rpm")) return "RPM"; return "Unknown"; } private string GetLinuxDistribution() { try { var osRelease = File.ReadAllLines("/etc/os-release") .Select(line => line.Split('=')) .Where(parts => parts.Length == 2) .ToDictionary(parts => parts[0], parts => parts[1].Trim('"')); return osRelease.GetValueOrDefault("PRETTY_NAME", "Unknown"); } catch { return "Unknown"; } } private bool HasUserConsent() { // Check if user has consented to telemetry return Preferences.Get("telemetry_enabled", false); } } ``` ## Release Checklist Before each release: - ✅ All tests passing (unit, integration, manual) - ✅ Version numbers updated (.csproj, changelog, manifests) - ✅ Release notes written - ✅ Packages built for all formats (.deb, .rpm, Flatpak, AppImage) - ✅ Packages tested on clean VMs - ✅ Code signing completed - ✅ Git tag created and pushed - ✅ GitHub/GitLab release created with artifacts - ✅ Distribution repositories updated (PPA, COPR, AUR) - ✅ Flathub pull request submitted (if applicable) - ✅ Documentation updated - ✅ Social media/blog announcement prepared A well-executed distribution strategy ensures your OpenMaui application reaches users across the diverse Linux ecosystem while maintaining quality and reliability. --- > With 217 passing unit tests covering views, services, and handlers, OpenMaui demonstrates that comprehensive testing is achievable even for complex platform implementations. > The beauty of AppImage is its simplicity: bundle everything your application needs into a single executable file that runs on virtually any Linux distribution without installation. > Testing in WSL provides convenience during development, but native Linux testing is essential for catching display server quirks, font rendering issues, and hardware acceleration problems.
subtitle: Take your OpenMaui Linux applications from development to production with comprehensive testing strategies, packaging for multiple distributions, and deployment best practices.
pullQuotes: With 217 passing unit tests covering views, services, and handlers, OpenMaui demonstrates that comprehensive testing is achievable even for complex platform implementations.
The beauty of AppImage is its simplicity: bundle everything your application needs into a single executable file that runs on virtually any Linux distribution without installation.
Testing in WSL provides convenience during development, but native Linux testing is essential for catching display server quirks, font rendering issues, and hardware acceleration problems.

sections.content:
Developing a .NET MAUI application for Linux is just the beginning. To deliver a professional, production-ready application, you need robust testing, proper packaging for different Linux distributions, and a streamlined deployment pipeline. OpenMaui's comprehensive Linux platform implementation demonstrates how to achieve all of this while maintaining 100% MAUI API compliance.

The journey from `dotnet run` to a polished application available in distribution repositories involves several critical steps: unit and integration testing of your Skia-based views, packaging for different distribution formats (.deb, .rpm, Flatpak, Snap, AppImage), setting up continuous integration pipelines, and establishing update mechanisms. Each step presents unique challenges in the Linux ecosystem, where fragmentation across distributions, display servers (X11 vs Wayland), and desktop environments (GNOME, KDE, XFCE) requires careful consideration.

This guide walks through the complete production pipeline for OpenMaui applications, drawing from real-world implementation experience with SkiaSharp rendering, GTK integration, and cross-distribution compatibility. Whether you're targeting a single distribution or aiming for universal Linux support, these strategies will help you deliver a reliable, maintainable application.

sections.content:
Debian (.deb) and RPM packages are the foundation of Linux distribution, used by Ubuntu/Debian and Fedora/RHEL families respectively. Proper packaging ensures your application integrates seamlessly with system package managers.

## Debian Package Structure

Create a `debian/` directory in your project root:

```
debian/
├── changelog
├── control
├── copyright
├── rules
├── openmaui-app.install
└── openmaui-app.desktop
```

**debian/control** - Package metadata:
```
Source: openmaui-app
Section: misc
Priority: optional
Maintainer: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
Build-Depends: debhelper (>= 12), dotnet-sdk-8.0
Standards-Version: 4.5.0

Package: openmaui-app
Architecture: amd64
Depends: ${shlibs:Depends}, ${misc:Depends},
         libgtk-3-0 (>= 3.24),
         libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37,
         libskia,
         libharfbuzz0b,
         aspnetcore-runtime-8.0
Description: OpenMaui Linux Application
 A cross-platform .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux
 with full Skia rendering and GTK integration.
```

**debian/rules** - Build instructions:
```makefile
#!/usr/bin/make -f

%:
	dh $@

override_dh_auto_build:
	dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \
		--self-contained false \
		-p:PublishSingleFile=false \
		-o debian/tmp/usr/lib/openmaui-app

override_dh_auto_install:
	dh_auto_install
	install -D -m 644 debian/openmaui-app.desktop \
		debian/openmaui-app/usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop
	install -D -m 644 Resources/appicon.png \
		debian/openmaui-app/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png
```

**debian/openmaui-app.install** - File installation paths:
```
usr/lib/openmaui-app/*
usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop
usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png
```

**debian/changelog** - Version history:
```
openmaui-app (1.0.0-1) unstable; urgency=medium

  * Initial release
  * Full MAUI API compliance
  * X11 and Wayland support

 -- Your Name <your.email@example.com>  Mon, 20 Jan 2025 10:00:00 +0000
```

## Building the .deb Package

```bash
# Install build tools
sudo apt-get install debhelper devscripts

# Build package
dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b

# Result: ../openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_amd64.deb

# Test installation
sudo dpkg -i ../openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_amd64.deb
sudo apt-get install -f  # Fix dependencies if needed
```

## RPM Package Structure

Create an RPM spec file `openmaui-app.spec`:

```spec
Name:           openmaui-app
Version:        1.0.0
Release:        1%{?dist}
Summary:        OpenMaui Linux Application

License:        MIT
URL:            https://github.com/yourusername/openmaui-app
Source0:        %{name}-%{version}.tar.gz

BuildRequires:  dotnet-sdk-8.0
Requires:       gtk3 >= 3.24
Requires:       webkit2gtk3
Requires:       aspnetcore-runtime-8.0
Requires:       libSkiaSharp
Requires:       harfbuzz

%description
A cross-platform .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux
with full Skia rendering and GTK integration.

%prep
%setup -q

%build
dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \
    --self-contained false \
    -p:PublishSingleFile=false \
    -o %{buildroot}/usr/lib/%{name}

%install
rm -rf %{buildroot}
mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/lib/%{name}
mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/share/applications
mkdir -p %{buildroot}/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps

install -m 644 openmaui-app.desktop %{buildroot}/usr/share/applications/
install -m 644 Resources/appicon.png %{buildroot}/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/%{name}.png

%files
/usr/lib/%{name}/

/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/%{name}.png

%changelog
* Mon Jan 20 2025 Your Name <your.email@example.com> - 1.0.0-1

## Dependency Management

OpenMaui applications require specific runtime dependencies:

**Core dependencies:**
- `aspnetcore-runtime-8.0` - .NET runtime
- `libgtk-3-0` / `gtk3` - GTK windowing
- `libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37` / `webkit2gtk3` - WebView support
- `libskia` - SkiaSharp native libraries
- `libharfbuzz0b` / `harfbuzz` - Text shaping

**Optional dependencies:**
- `libva2` - Hardware video acceleration
- `at-spi2-core` - Accessibility support
- `ibus` / `fcitx5` - Input method support

Test your packages on clean VMs to verify all dependencies are correctly specified.

Flatpak and Snap provide sandboxed, distribution-agnostic packaging with automatic updates. They're ideal for reaching users across all Linux distributions without maintaining separate .deb and .rpm packages. ## Flatpak Packaging Flatpak uses manifest files to define your application and its dependencies. **com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml** - Flatpak manifest: ```yaml app-id: com.example.OpenMauiApp runtime: org.freedesktop.Platform runtime-version: '23.08' sdk: org.freedesktop.Sdk sdk-extensions: - org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.dotnet8 command: openmaui-app finish-args: # Wayland and X11 access - --socket=wayland - --socket=fallback-x11 - --share=ipc # GPU acceleration - --device=dri # File system access - --filesystem=home - --filesystem=xdg-documents # Network access (for WebView) - --share=network # Desktop integration - --talk-name=org.freedesktop.Notifications - --talk-name=org.freedesktop.secrets - --talk-name=org.gtk.vfs.* # Theme access - --filesystem=xdg-config/gtk-3.0:ro - --env=GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark build-options: append-path: /usr/lib/sdk/dotnet8/bin env: PKG_CONFIG_PATH: /app/lib/pkgconfig:/app/share/pkgconfig:/usr/lib/pkgconfig:/usr/share/pkgconfig modules: # SkiaSharp native dependencies - name: skia buildsystem: simple build-commands: - install -Dm755 libSkiaSharp.so /app/lib/libSkiaSharp.so sources: - type: file url: https://www.nuget.org/api/v2/package/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux/2.88.9 sha256: # HarfBuzz for text shaping - name: harfbuzz buildsystem: meson config-opts: - -Dglib=enabled - -Dfreetype=enabled sources: - type: archive url: https://github.com/harfbuzz/harfbuzz/releases/download/7.3.0/harfbuzz-7.3.0.tar.xz sha256: # Main application - name: openmaui-app buildsystem: simple build-commands: - dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained false -o /app/bin - install -Dm644 com.example.OpenMauiApp.desktop /app/share/applications/com.example.OpenMauiApp.desktop - install -Dm644 Resources/appicon.png /app/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/com.example.OpenMauiApp.png - install -Dm644 com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml /app/share/metainfo/com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml sources: - type: dir path: . ``` **Building and testing Flatpak:** ```bash # Install flatpak-builder sudo apt-get install flatpak-builder # Add Flathub repository flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo # Install required runtimes flatpak install flathub org.freedesktop.Platform//23.08 flatpak install flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk//23.08 flatpak install flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.dotnet8//23.08 # Build Flatpak flatpak-builder --force-clean build-dir com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml # Test locally flatpak-builder --run build-dir com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml openmaui-app # Create single-file bundle for distribution flatpak-builder --repo=repo --force-clean build-dir com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml flatpak build-bundle repo openmaui-app.flatpak com.example.OpenMauiApp # Install bundle flatpak install openmaui-app.flatpak ``` **AppStream metadata** (com.example.OpenMauiApp.metainfo.xml): ```xml com.example.OpenMauiApp CC0-1.0 MIT OpenMaui App Cross-platform .NET MAUI application

A production-ready .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux with full Skia rendering, GTK integration, and cross-platform compatibility.

https://example.com/screenshots/main.png https://example.com ``` ## Snap Packaging Snap uses a `snapcraft.yaml` file for configuration. **snapcraft.yaml:** ```yaml name: openmaui-app base: core22 version: '1.0.0' summary: OpenMaui Linux Application description: | A cross-platform .NET MAUI application running natively on Linux with full Skia rendering and GTK integration. grade: stable confinement: strict apps: openmaui-app: command: bin/openmaui-app extensions: [gnome] plugs: - home - network - desktop - desktop-legacy - wayland - x11 - opengl - audio-playback - removable-media parts: dotnet-runtime: plugin: nil build-packages: - wget override-build: | wget https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh chmod +x dotnet-install.sh ./dotnet-install.sh --channel 8.0 --runtime aspnetcore --install-dir $SNAPCRAFT_PART_INSTALL/dotnet stage: - dotnet/* openmaui-app: plugin: dotnet source: . dotnet-build-configuration: Release dotnet-self-contained-runtime-identifier: linux-x64 build-packages: - dotnet-sdk-8.0 stage-packages: - libgtk-3-0 - libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 - libharfbuzz0b - libfontconfig1 - libfreetype6 override-build: | dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \ --self-contained false \ -o $SNAPCRAFT_PART_INSTALL/bin ``` **Building and publishing Snap:** ```bash # Install snapcraft sudo snap install snapcraft --classic # Build snap snapcraft # Test locally sudo snap install openmaui-app_1.0.0_amd64.snap --dangerous # Run application openmaui-app # Publish to Snap Store (requires account) snapcraft login snapcraft upload openmaui-app_1.0.0_amd64.snap --release=stable ``` ## Portal Permissions Both Flatpak and Snap use portals for secure file access: ```csharp // OpenMaui handles portals automatically public class LinuxFilePicker : IFilePicker { public async Task PickAsync(PickOptions options) { // Detects if running in sandbox if (IsRunningInFlatpak() || IsRunningInSnap()) { // Use xdg-desktop-portal for sandboxed file access return await PickViaPortalAsync(options); } else { // Direct file picker (zenity/kdialog) return await PickDirectAsync(options); } } private bool IsRunningInFlatpak() { return File.Exists("/.flatpak-info"); } private bool IsRunningInSnap() { return !string.IsNullOrEmpty(Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("SNAP")); } } ``` ## Comparison: Flatpak vs Snap **Flatpak advantages:** - Better desktop integration - More granular permissions - Preferred by traditional Linux distributions - Excellent for GTK applications **Snap advantages:** - Simpler manifest syntax - Better CLI tool support - Strong Ubuntu/Canonical ecosystem - Automatic delta updates For OpenMaui applications, **Flatpak is recommended** due to superior GTK integration and broader community adoption.

AppImage provides the simplest distribution method: a single executable file that runs on any Linux distribution without installation. The beauty of AppImage is its simplicity: bundle everything your application needs into a single executable file that runs on virtually any Linux distribution without installation.

## Creating an AppImage

AppImages use the AppDir structure with all dependencies bundled:

```bash
# Install appimagetool
wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage
chmod +x appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage

# Create AppDir structure
mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/bin
mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/lib
mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/share/applications
mkdir -p OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps
```

## Publishing Self-Contained Build

Publish your application with all dependencies:

```bash
dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 \
  --self-contained true \
  -p:PublishSingleFile=false \
  -p:PublishTrimmed=false \
  -o publish/

# Copy to AppDir
cp -r publish/* OpenMauiApp.AppDir/usr/bin/
```

## Bundling Native Dependencies

Copy required native libraries:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# bundle-dependencies.sh

APPDIR="OpenMauiApp.AppDir"
LIBDIR="$APPDIR/usr/lib"

# Copy GTK and dependencies
cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgtk-3.so.0 $LIBDIR/
cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgdk-3.so.0 $LIBDIR/
cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libwebkit2gtk-4.0.so.37 $LIBDIR/

# Copy SkiaSharp native
cp ~/.nuget/packages/skiasharp.nativeassets.linux/2.88.9/runtimes/linux-x64/native/libSkiaSharp.so $LIBDIR/

# Copy HarfBuzz
cp /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libharfbuzz.so.0 $LIBDIR/

# Copy their dependencies
for lib in $LIBDIR/*.so*; do
    ldd $lib | grep "=> /" | awk '{print $3}' | while read dep; do
        if [ ! -f "$LIBDIR/$(basename $dep)" ]; then
            cp $dep $LIBDIR/
        fi
    done
done
```

## AppRun Script

Create the launcher script `OpenMauiApp.AppDir/AppRun`:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Get the directory of this script
HERE="$(dirname "$(readlink -f "${0}")")"

# Set library path to include bundled libraries
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"

# Set GTK theme path
export GTK_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib/gtk-3.0"
export GTK_DATA_PREFIX="$HERE/usr"

# Set GDK backend (prefer Wayland, fallback to X11)
export GDK_BACKEND=wayland,x11

# Disable AppImage desktop integration (optional)
export APPIMAGE_DISABLE_INTEGRATION=1

# Run the application
exec "$HERE/usr/bin/OpenMauiApp" "$@"
```

Make it executable:

chmod +x OpenMauiApp.AppDir/AppRun

## Runtime Update Checks

Implement update checking in your application:

```csharp
public class AppImageUpdateService
{
    public async Task<bool> CheckForUpdatesAsync()
    {
        var appImagePath = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("APPIMAGE");
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(appImagePath))
            return false; // Not running as AppImage
        
        var process = new Process
        {
            StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo
            {
                FileName = "appimageupdatetool",
                Arguments = $"-j {appImagePath}",
                RedirectStandardOutput = true,
                UseShellExecute = false
            }
        };
        
        process.Start();
        var output = await process.StandardOutput.ReadToEndAsync();
        await process.WaitForExitAsync();
        
        var updateInfo = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<UpdateInfo>(output);
        return updateInfo?.UpdateAvailable ?? false;
    }
    
    public async Task ApplyUpdateAsync()
    {
        var appImagePath = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("APPIMAGE");
        var process = new Process
        {
            StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo
            {
                FileName = "appimageupdatetool",
                Arguments = appImagePath,
                UseShellExecute = true
            }
        };
        
        process.Start();
        await process.WaitForExitAsync();
        
        // Restart application
        Process.Start(appImagePath);
        Environment.Exit(0);
    }
}
```

## Automated AppImage Build Script

Create `build-appimage.sh`:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

VERSION="1.0.0"
APPNAME="OpenMauiApp"
APPDIR="$APPNAME.AppDir"

echo "Building $APPNAME v$VERSION AppImage..."

# Clean previous build
rm -rf $APPDIR
rm -f $APPNAME-*.AppImage

# Publish application
dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained true -o publish/

# Create AppDir structure
mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/bin
mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/lib
mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/share/applications
mkdir -p $APPDIR/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps

# Copy application
cp -r publish/* $APPDIR/usr/bin/

# Bundle dependencies
./bundle-dependencies.sh

# Copy desktop entry and icon
cp openmaui-app.desktop $APPDIR/usr/share/applications/
cp Resources/appicon.png $APPDIR/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/openmaui-app.png
cp Resources/appicon.png $APPDIR/openmaui-app.png
cp Resources/appicon.png $APPDIR/.DirIcon
ln -s usr/share/applications/openmaui-app.desktop $APPDIR/openmaui-app.desktop

# Create AppRun
cat > $APPDIR/AppRun << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
HERE="$(dirname "$(readlink -f "${0}")")" 
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"
export GTK_PATH="$HERE/usr/lib/gtk-3.0"
exec "$HERE/usr/bin/OpenMauiApp" "$@"
EOF
chmod +x $APPDIR/AppRun

# Build AppImage
./appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage $APPDIR $APPNAME-$VERSION-x86_64.AppImage

echo "AppImage created: $APPNAME-$VERSION-x86_64.AppImage"
echo "Size: $(du -h $APPNAME-$VERSION-x86_64.AppImage | cut -f1)"
```

Automated build, test, and release pipelines ensure consistent quality and streamline distribution across multiple package formats. Here's how to set up comprehensive CI/CD for OpenMaui Linux applications.

## GitHub Actions Workflow

Create `.github/workflows/release.yml`:

```yaml
name: Build and Release

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags:
      - 'v*'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  DOTNET_VERSION: '8.0.x'
  PROJECT_NAME: OpenMauiApp

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3
        with:
          dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y xvfb libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 \
            libharfbuzz0b libfontconfig1 libfreetype6
      
      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore
      
      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build -c Release --no-restore
      
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: dotnet test -c Release --no-build --verbosity normal \
          --filter Category=Unit
      
      - name: Run integration tests
        run: xvfb-run -a dotnet test -c Release --no-build --verbosity normal \
          --filter Category=Integration

  build-deb:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3
        with:
          dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Install build tools
        run: sudo apt-get install -y debhelper devscripts
      
      - name: Build .deb package
        run: |
          dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b
          mv ../*.deb .
      
      - name: Upload .deb artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        with:
          name: deb-package
          path: '*.deb'

  build-rpm:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    container:
      image: fedora:latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          dnf install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0 rpm-build rpmdevtools
      
      - name: Setup RPM build tree
        run: rpmdev-setuptree
      
      - name: Build RPM
        run: |
          cp openmaui-app.spec ~/rpmbuild/SPECS/
          tar czf ~/rpmbuild/SOURCES/${{ env.PROJECT_NAME }}-${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}.tar.gz .
          rpmbuild -ba ~/rpmbuild/SPECS/openmaui-app.spec
          cp ~/rpmbuild/RPMS/x86_64/*.rpm .
      
      - name: Upload RPM artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        with:
          name: rpm-package
          path: '*.rpm'

  build-flatpak:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Install Flatpak and flatpak-builder
        run: |
          sudo apt-get install -y flatpak flatpak-builder
          flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo
          flatpak install -y flathub org.freedesktop.Platform//23.08
          flatpak install -y flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk//23.08
          flatpak install -y flathub org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.dotnet8//23.08
      
      - name: Build Flatpak
        run: |
          flatpak-builder --repo=repo --force-clean build-dir \
            com.example.OpenMauiApp.yml
          flatpak build-bundle repo ${{ env.PROJECT_NAME }}.flatpak \
            com.example.OpenMauiApp
      
      - name: Upload Flatpak artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        with:
          name: flatpak-package
          path: '*.flatpak'

  build-appimage:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v3
        with:
          dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get install -y libgtk-3-0 libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 \
            libharfbuzz0b zsync
          wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage
          chmod +x appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage
      
      - name: Build AppImage
        run: |
          chmod +x build-appimage.sh
          ./build-appimage.sh
          zsyncmake ${{ env.PROJECT_NAME }}-*.AppImage
      
      - name: Upload AppImage artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        with:
          name: appimage-package
          path: |
            *.AppImage
            *.AppImage.zsync

  release:
    needs: [build-deb, build-rpm, build-flatpak, build-appimage]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    steps:
      - name: Download all artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v3
      
      - name: Create Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          files: |
            deb-package/*.deb
            rpm-package/*.rpm
            flatpak-package/*.flatpak
            appimage-package/*.AppImage
            appimage-package/*.AppImage.zsync
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

```bash
# GPG signing for .deb packages
dpkg-sig --sign builder openmaui-app_1.0.0-1_amd64.deb

# RPM signing
rpmsign --addsign openmaui-app-1.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm

# AppImage signing
gpg --detach-sign --armor OpenMauiApp-1.0.0-x86_64.AppImage
```

Store signing keys in CI secrets and automate signing in the pipeline.
